supersede_reason: EXP-268 ID collision. The previous proposal (proposal-ISS-1578-resolution.json) referenced findings_file EXP-268-iss-1578-citation-fix.json, which was renumbered to EXP-269 on 2026-04-27T19:53Z by the analyst. The c1 follow-up tracker entry for EXP-260 (covering ISS-1613/1614/1615) was registered first (18:21:37Z) and retained the EXP-268 ID; the citation-fix was renumbered to resolve the collision. Decider: please use EXP-269-iss-1578-citation-fix.json as the findings file. The orphaned EXP-268-iss-1578-citation-fix.json is FUSE-immortal and should be ignored.
summary: ISS-1578 (citation fix in §4.2.7 trilemma Geometric horn) — single-substring patch on data/sections.json part4.html. Replaces inaccurate formula_runtime.json citation with status_history.json citation naming the dome's solar-diameter / lunar-diameter scored domains as the dome itself names them (Solar Angular Diameter, Moon Angular Diameter Variation). Live-fetch verification on 2026-04-27 confirmed: formula_runtime.json (V51.1) contains 6 formula records and zero solar/lunar-diameter entries; status_history.json's most-recent entry contains 39 scored domains including the cited two. Anchor verified count=1 in current sections.json; replacement preserves <li> and sentence structure. Net delta +93 chars. After decider integrates EXP-269, ISS-1578 closes.
side_note_for_decider: EXP-269 other_defects_noted flags a separate scope-out: §4.1 is titled "The 28-Domain / 47.69σ Dashboard" but the dome's status_history.json now reports 39 scored domains. The 47.69σ figure may also be stale. Recommended as a fresh ISS for the decider to spawn — analyst-substantive work, deliberately scoped out of EXP-269 to keep this a clean citation fix.
